# Review: `deepmind/867.lean` — Erdős Problem 867

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 867`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/867`.
- [ ] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
  - **Issue:** The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and two bibliography entries (`[Er92c]`, `[CoPh96]`). The actual problem description appears only in the theorem docstring. The checklist requires the module docstring itself to include a brief plain-English summary.
- [x] Optional bibliography entries present (`[Er92c]`, `[CoPh96]`).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos867 ... end Erdos867`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos867`).
- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `ConsecutiveSumFree` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] No classical choice or nonconstructive operations — `noncomputable` not needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Type uses `UpperCamelCase`: `ConsecutiveSumFree`.
- N/A: No function definitions returning types (no `lowerCamelCase` check needed).
- N/A: No `local notation` needed.
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_867` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er92c]` and `[CoPh96]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is it true that …?" — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P`, correct for a disproved yes/no question.
- [x] `answer(False)` is used (problem was disproved by Coppersmith and Phillips).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (`∃ C, ∀ N, ∀ A, … A.card ≤ N / 2 + C`), so `answer(False)` correctly means "no."
- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ C`, `∀ N`, `∀ A`) appear **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category research solved]`.
- [x] `research solved` is a valid category value.
- N/A: Not `research open`, so the `sorry`-free proof check does not apply.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 5 11]` attribute (not multiple separate `@[AMS]` tags).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5, 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (acceptable multi-line form for open/unverified proofs).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] Theorem docstring describes the known disproof — this is appropriate for a `research solved` problem (the "no informal proof sketches" rule applies only to `research open` problems).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. Single-theorem file.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `∉`, `⊆`, `ℕ`, `≤`, `∑`, `↔`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified (requires manual build).
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified (requires manual build).

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS with one minor issue**

The file is well-structured and conforms to nearly all checklist items. The formalization is clean: `ConsecutiveSumFree` is correctly defined with a docstring, the main theorem uses the correct `answer(False) ↔ P` pattern for a disproved yes/no question, and attributes are properly formatted.

**Issue to address:**
1. **Module docstring missing problem description (§3):** The module docstring should include a brief plain-English summary of the problem (e.g., "Erdős asked whether a consecutive-sum-free subset of {1,…,N} can have at most N/2 + C elements"). Currently the description appears only in the theorem docstring.

    53→$$

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 867

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/867](https://www.erdosproblems.com/867)

[Er92c] Erdős, P., _Some of my favourite problems which recently have been solved_. Proceedings
of the international conference on set-theoretic topology and its applications, Part 2, Matsuyama
(1992).

[CoPh96] Coppersmith, D. and Phillips, S., _Sums along the rationals_. Preprint (1996).
-/

open Finset

namespace Erdos867

/-- A finite set $A$ of natural numbers is "consecutive-sum-free" if, when its
elements are listed in increasing order as $a_1 < a_2 < \cdots < a_t$, no sum of
two or more consecutive elements ($a_i + a_{i+1} + \cdots + a_j$ with $i < j$)
lies in $A$. -/
def ConsecutiveSumFree (A : Finset ℕ) : Prop :=
  let s := A.sort (· ≤ ·)
  ∀ i j : ℕ, i < j → j < s.length →
    (∑ k ∈ Finset.Icc i j, s.getD k 0) ∉ A

/--
Erdős Problem 867 (disproved) [Er92c]:

Is it true that if $A = \{a_1 < \cdots < a_t\} \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ has no sum of two or
more consecutive elements in $A$, then $|A| \le N/2 + C$ for some absolute constant $C$?

Disproved by Coppersmith and Phillips [CoPh96], who proved the maximum $|A|$ satisfies
$$
  \frac{13}{24} N - O(1) \le |A| \le \left(\frac{2}{3} - \frac{1}{512}\right) N + \log N.
$$
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
theorem erdos_867 : answer(False) ↔
    ∃ C : ℕ, ∀ N : ℕ, ∀ A : Finset ℕ, A ⊆ Finset.Icc 1 N →
    ConsecutiveSumFree A →
    A.card ≤ N / 2 + C := by
  sorry

end Erdos867

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/867](https://www.erdosproblems.com/867)

Erdős asked whether a consecutive-sum-free subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ can have at most
$N/2 + C$ elements for some absolute constant $C$. This was disproved by Coppersmith and Phillips.

[Er92c]

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 867

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/867](https://www.erdosproblems.com/867)

[Er92c] Erdős, P., _Some of my favourite problems which recently have been solved_. Proceedings
of the international conference on set-theoretic topology and its applications, Part 2, Matsuyama
(1992).

[CoPh96] Coppersmith, D. and Phillips, S., _Sums along the rationals_. Preprint (1996).
-/

open Finset

namespace Erdos867

/-- A finite set $A$ of natural numbers is "consecutive-sum-free" if, when its
elements are listed in increasing order as $a_1 < a_2 < \cdots < a_t$, no sum of
two or more consecutive elements ($a_i + a_{i+1} + \cdots + a_j$ with $i < j$)
lies in $A$. -/
def ConsecutiveSumFree (A : Finset ℕ) : Prop :=
  let s := A.sort (· ≤ ·)
  ∀ i j : ℕ, i < j → j < s.length →
    (∑ k ∈ Finset.Icc i j, s.getD k 0) ∉ A

/--
Erdős Problem 867 (disproved) [Er92c]:

Is it true that if $A = \{a_1 < \cdots < a_t\} \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ has no sum of two or
more consecutive elements in $A$, then $|A| \le N/2 + C$ for some absolute constant $C$?

Disproved by Coppersmith and Phillips [CoPh96], who proved the maximum $|A|$ satisfies
$$
  \frac{13}{24} N - O(1) \le |A| \le \left(\frac{2}{3} - \frac{1}{512}\right) N + \log N.
$$
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
theorem erdos_867 : answer(False) ↔
    ∃ C : ℕ, ∀ N : ℕ, ∀ A : Finset ℕ, A ⊆ Finset.Icc 1 N →
    ConsecutiveSumFree A →
    A.card ≤ N / 2 + C := by
  sorry

end Erdos867

lines: +Erdős asked whether a consecutive-sum-free subset of $\{1, \ldots, N\}$ can have at most
+$N/2 + C$ elements for some absolute constant $C$. This was disproved by Coppersmith and Phillips.
[Er92c] Erdős, P., _Some of my favourite problems which recently have been solved_. Proceedings
of the international conference on set-theoretic topology and its applications, Part 2, Matsuyama
